item.title: Correction on 9231 Further Pure Mathematics 1
item.description:
The PDF has been updated to correct a mistaken equation in the first page on roots of polynomials.

Git show: 
commit 009a0c338e7c1c8564a6c95e840d746e6c8a9e50 (HEAD -> master, origin/master)
Author: acutewoof 
Date:   Sun Apr 19 07:37:08 2026 +0530

    correct d -> 3d

diff --git a/pure-1.typ b/pure-1.typ
index 1109cae..a3b1457 100644
--- a/pure-1.typ
+++ b/pure-1.typ
@@ -52,7 +52,7 @@ $
 $
   a (alpha^3 + beta^3 + gamma^3) + b (alpha^2 + beta^2 + gamma^2) + c (alpha + beta + gamma) + 3d = 0
 $
-$ a S_3 + b S_2 + c S_1 + d = 0 $
+$ a S_3 + b S_2 + c S_1 + 3d = 0 $
 
 It should be evident from this example how this can be applied to other
 polynomials

item.title: Correction on 9709 Pure Mathematics 2 and 3
item.description:
The PDF has been updated to correct a mistaken line under the section for differential equations: the last equation stated, integral f(y) dy = integral g(y) dx; it should state, integral f(y) dy = integral g(x) dx.

Git show: 
commit 51e1609349717db598f0b758099ba8bc77b400bc (HEAD -> master, origin/master)
Author: acutewoof 
Date:   Sat Apr 18 16:19:39 2026 +0530

    fixed mistyped g(y) in place of g(x)

diff --git a/pure-math-2-3.typ b/pure-math-2-3.typ
index fa00f31..0a37970 100644
--- a/pure-math-2-3.typ
+++ b/pure-math-2-3.typ
@@ -118,7 +118,7 @@ $ integral (k f'(x))/f(x) dif x = k ln(abs(f(x))) + c $
 
 Differential equations in this syllabus can be solved by integrating both sides of the equation once rearranged such that the equation is of the form $f(y)(dif y)/(dif x) = g(x)$.
 
-$ f(y)(dif y)/(dif x) = g(x) => integral f(y) (dif y)/(dif x)dif x = integral g(x) dif x => integral f(y) dif y = integral g(y) dif x $
+$ f(y)(dif y)/(dif x) = g(x) => integral f(y) (dif y)/(dif x)dif x = integral g(x) dif x => integral f(y) dif y = integral g(x) dif x $
 
The general solution to a differential equation doesn't consider example points on the graph to find the constants of integration. The particular solution does.
